title: Include ready-to-run follow-up command hint with every suggestion
description:
When any suggestion is triggered on a create or update command, the output includes a ready-to-run follow-up command that the caller can execute immediately to update the content. The command has the issue ID (or issue ID + comment ID) already filled in, so the caller only needs to supply the improved content.

This is the most actionable form of a suggestion: it reduces the friction of acting on the hint to a single copy-paste-edit loop.

Scenario: Suggestion after creating an issue includes an update hint

Given a suggestion is triggered when an agent creates issue tskl-xyz
When the suggestion is printed to stderr
Then it includes a hint of the form:
  To update the description: kbs update tskl-xyz --description "<your improved description here>"

Scenario: Suggestion after adding a comment includes a comment update hint

Given a suggestion is triggered when an agent adds comment abc123 to issue tskl-xyz
When the suggestion is printed to stderr
Then it includes a hint of the form:
  To update this comment: kbs comment update tskl-xyz abc123 "<your improved comment here>"

Scenario: Suggestion after updating a description includes a re-update hint

Given a suggestion is triggered when an agent updates the description of tskl-xyz
Then the hint uses the same kbs update tskl-xyz --description form

Scenario: Multiple suggestions share one hint block

Given both the Markdown suggestion and the diagram suggestion are triggered for the same create operation
Then they are grouped together in the output
And a single update hint is shown once at the bottom of the suggestion block rather than repeated per suggestion

Scenario: The hint is clearly delineated from suggestion text

Given suggestions and their hint are printed
Then the hint line is visually distinct (e.g. indented or prefixed with a right arrow or "Hint:")
And it is easy to identify where the command begins and ends
creator: ryan.porter
